name: Leo Parker
abstract: Leo Parker (April 18, 1925 – February 11, 1962) was an American jazz musician, who primarily played baritone saxophone. Parker was the earliest baritone saxophonist to play bebop.

concerts.label: Fred Robbins One-Nite Stand: Illinois Jacquet and His Orchestra with Ella Fitzgerald

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Leo Parker was playing alto saxophone on that... [in Coleman Hawkins' band] ... Leo Parker was here then, because he had left the Eckstine band, and Leo came up with his baritone and sat in with... I furnished the rhythm section for them.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: That was the greatest band I ever played with in my life, the band that Billy Eckstine had... They had Charlie Parker, Lucky Thompson, Dexter Gordon, Leo Parker... The trumpets, they had me and Dizzy

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: A little later I got to play with Leo Parker and guys like that, because they lived there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ira Gitler
about_quotes.evidence: Dizzy opened at the Spotlight Club in 1946... with a small band with Leo Parker on baritone... And I went down to hear them. And you know it just knocked me out.
